section_name: Addition of Political Subdivision or Other Public Entity to District
text:
The following political subdivisions and other public entities may become a part of a district with the approval of the governing body of the political subdivision or public entity:

(1) a county located adjacent to the county in which a creating municipality is located;

(2) a municipality with a population of more than 18,000 located in a county described by Subdivision (1); and

(3) a public entity located in a county that has become part of the district.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 5
### Chapter 173. Intermunicipal Commuter Rail Districts
##### § 173.052 — Addition of Political Subdivision or Other Public Entity to District

The following political subdivisions and other public entities may become a part of a district with the approval of the governing body of the political subdivision or public entity:

    (1) a county located adjacent to the county in which a creating municipality is located;

    (2) a municipality with a population of more than 18,000 located in a county described by Subdivision (1); and

    (3) a public entity located in a county that has become part of the district.

*Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 91 (S.B.1303), Sec. 24.003(a), eff. September 1, 2011.*

subsections.text: The following political subdivisions and other public entities may become a part of a district with the approval of the governing body of the political subdivision or public entity:
a county located adjacent to the county in which a creating municipality is located;
a municipality with a population of more than 18,000 located in a county described by Subdivision (1); and
a public entity located in a county that has become part of the district.
